content:
<act>
<tool>graph.plan</tool>
<objective>Create a SF novel with detailed settings</objective>
<constraints>novel length 80k words, three acts, world-building depth 4 levels, character arcs complete, no plot holes</constraints>
<assumptions>author has writing capability and can iterate on drafts</assumptions>
<risks>world-building may require multiple passes to achieve consistency</risks>
<steps>1. Define core setting pillars (time period, geography, magic system, political factions) 2. Create character profiles with motivations and flaws 3. Outline three-act structure with turning points 4. Write act I: exposition and inciting incident 5. Write act II: rising action and mid-point twist 6. Write act III: climax and resolution</steps>
<checks>world consistency, character motivation alignment, pacing rhythm, theme coherence</checks>
<paths>stories/sf-novel-with-detailed-settings/01-world-pillars.md, stories/sf-novel-with-detailed-settings/02-characters.md, stories/sf-novel-with-detailed-settings/03-outline.md, stories/sf-novel-with-detailed-settings/drafts</paths>
<reason>Structured planning ensures world-building depth and narrative coherence before drafting begins</reason>
</act>
